deep_dive.title: Closing the Activation-Cone Blind Spot: Response-Time Probing and Unified Defense
deep_dive.abstract: Inference-time safety methods for large language models have proliferated, yet no systematic comparison exists. We evaluate five defense paradigms (no defense, static steering, CAST, AlphaSteer, probe-gated) across seven instruction-tuned models (7-31B) and five attack types (GCG, AutoDAN, DeepInception, prefilling, intent laundering). Our central finding: prompt-time activation defenses are structurally blind to prefilling attacks. AlphaSteer achieves 0% attack success on GCG, AutoDAN, and intent laundering but 50% on prefilling. We prove a corollary: any defense that gates intervention on a single layer's activation alignment with a benign reference (cone, subspace, or null-space) is blind to attacks that craft activations to lie inside that reference, whether checked at prompt time or per token. As its constructive contrapositive we introduce response-time probing: a linear probe on the model's hidden state at the first generated tokens, with AUROC 0.97-1.00 across all seven models. Combined with a halt, it cuts prefilling attack success to 0/40 on every model with 0% benign false positives, outperforming Llama Guard 3. Cross-template generalisation depends on probe depth, so we scope the claim to the canonical prefilling-template family. Composing the response-halt with AlphaSteer's null-space steering gives an orthogonal split (the halt catches prefilling, AlphaSteer catches semantic attacks), reaching defense success 0.983 on Mistral and 0.994 on Llama and dominating both components. We further show MMLU fails to capture steering's true utility cost, which appears as behavioral hedging rather than factual loss, and that diverse negative training sets cut probe false positives from 80-100% to near zero. Code, attacks, per-sample results, and the judge prompt are released.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes unified defense against prompt injection and adversarial prompts
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出针对提示注入和对抗性提示的统一防御
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出针对提示注入和对抗性提示的统一防御
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出针对提示注入和对抗性提示的统一防御
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Evaluates five inference-time defense paradigms against five attack types including prefilling and GCG, finding prompt-time activation defenses blind to prefilling; proposes AlphaSteer achieving 0% attack success on most attacks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文系统比较了五种推理时LLM安全防御范式（无防御、静态引导、CAST、AlphaSteer、探针门控）在七个模型上对抗五种攻击（GCG、AutoDAN、DeepInception、预填充、意图清洗）的效果。核心发现：基于提示时激活的防御对预填充攻击存在结构性盲区。提出的AlphaSteer防御在GCG、AutoDAN和意图清洗上实现0%攻击成功率，但对预填充攻击仅50%。该工作为防御提示注入提供了统一的比较框架和有效方法。

deep_dive.title: Janus: a Playground for User-Involved Agentic Permission Management
deep_dive.abstract: AI agents that autonomously execute tool calls on a user's behalf raise pressing questions about permission management: what role could users play, and what role should they play? Despite many proposed approaches, the user's role in agentic permission management remains under explored. We introduce Janus, a playground system for implementing and evaluating user-involved agentic permission management designs. Janus consists of two components: Janus-Core, a modular agentic system supporting a diverse spectrum of permission management designs, and Janus-Harness, an automated evaluation framework. Grounded in a conceptual model that identifies key design axes for user involvement, we implement six permission assistants spanning the design space and evaluate them across three scenarios and three synthetic responders. We demonstrate that user input is critical and can significantly strengthen privacy and security, that AI augmentation of user decisions can help reduce cognitive load, and that realistic user behavior including permission fatigue must be accounted for in system design. No single design performs optimally across all contexts, motivating a more principled and context-sensitive approach to deploying permission assistants in agentic systems. Janus is publicly available to support future investigation into this dimension of agentic system design.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Permission management system for AI agents, directly addressing safety mechanisms for autonomous agents
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对AI智能体的权限管理系统，直接涉及自主智能体的安全机制
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对AI智能体的权限管理系统，直接涉及自主智能体的安全机制
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对AI智能体的权限管理系统，直接涉及自主智能体的安全机制
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Janus provides a playground for user-involved agentic permission management, enabling evaluation of diverse safety designs for autonomous LLM agents.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 随着AI智能体自主执行工具调用，权限管理成为关键安全问题，但用户角色的设计空间尚未充分探索。本文提出Janus系统，包含模块化智能体核心和自动评估框架，实现了多种用户参与权限管理模式。实验基于概念模型实现了六个权限助手，展示了设计空间的覆盖范围。该工作为构建安全的自主智能体提供了可扩展的测试平台和设计指南。

quick_skim.title: A-TMA: Decoupling State-Aware Memory Failures in Long-Term Agent Memory
quick_skim.abstract: Long term memory lets LLM agents act as persistent assistants, but user facts change. A useful memory system must know what is true now, what used to be true, and what changed. We study \emph{ghost memory}, a state coordination failure in which old, current, and transition facts coexist in the memory bank, remain mixed during retrieval, and mislead the answer model. We argue that memory systems should be understood and optimized from three levels: bank maintenance, retrieval, and answer time resolution. We propose ATMA, a state aware overlay for existing memory systems. ATMA keeps superseded and transition records in the bank, builds evidence packets for the query's requested state view, and exposes current, historical, and transition labels to QA. We further call for decoupled evaluation of bank, retrieval, and answer level failures, since final QA accuracy can hide where ghost memory occurs. To make this failure measurable, we build LTP (LoCoMo Temporal Plus), a conflict heavy benchmark for ghost memory, and evaluate on LoCoMo for long conversation generalization. On LTP, Graphiti+ATMA improves conflict accuracy by 0.240 absolute over Graphiti. On LoCoMo, Graphiti+ATMA raises temporal F1 from 0.0295 to 0.1705. The gains are host dependent, but they indicate that explicit state roles can reduce memory failures hidden by final QA accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: improves safety of LLM agent memory by addressing state coordination failures
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过解决状态协调失败提高LLM智能体记忆的安全性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过解决状态协调失败提高LLM智能体记忆的安全性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过解决状态协调失败提高LLM智能体记忆的安全性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes A-TMA, a state-aware memory overlay for LLM agents that distinguishes current, historical, and transition facts, reducing ghost memory failures.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对LLM智能体长期记忆中的“幽灵记忆”问题（旧、当前、过渡事实混合误导回答），提出A-TMA状态感知覆盖层。A-TMA在记忆库中保留废弃和过渡记录，为查询构建证据包，并暴露当前、历史和过渡标签。实验表明该方法有效减少状态协调失败，提升智能体记忆可靠性，对智能体安全有重要意义。

quick_skim.title: Breaking the Rounding Trap: Securing LLMs against Quantization-Conditioned Backdoors
quick_skim.abstract: Model quantization is a key technique for reducing storage and inference costs when deploying large language models in practice. However, recent studies show that the discretization and rounding errors introduced by quantization can be exploited by adversaries to construct quantization-conditioned backdoor (QCB) attacks. Under such attacks, malicious behaviors remain dormant in the full-precision stage and are activated only after quantized deployment, thereby bypassing conventional security auditing and detection mechanisms. To address this threat, we propose a proactive pre-quantization defense method, QuantGuard. Our method introduces differentiable rounding control variables and combines error-guided rounding reversal constraints, output-distribution consistency, and weight-distance regularization to finely regulate critical rounding behaviors. Crucially, QuantGuard utilizes only a small calibration dataset and does not modify existing quantization algorithms. This design breaks the precise alignment between attacker-crafted weight patterns and quantization boundaries, effectively suppressing the post-quantization backdoor activation pathway while preserving the model's original functionality and performance. We conduct systematic experiments on six mainstream LLMs (including the LLaMA-3 and Qwen2.5-Coder) using three quantization precisions (INT8, FP4, and NF4) across three representative scenarios: vulnerable code generation, content injection, and over-refusal. The results show that QuantGuard consistently mitigates QCB attacks, reducing the attack success rate to a level comparable to the clean model while largely preserving performance on general capability benchmarks. With low computational overhead, our method offers an effective, practical solution for secure quantized LLM deployment.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes QuantGuard, a pre-quantization defense against quantization-conditioned backdoors
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出量化前防御方法QuantGuard，抵御量化后门攻击
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出量化前防御方法QuantGuard，抵御量化后门攻击
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出量化前防御方法QuantGuard，抵御量化后门攻击
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: QuantGuard introduces differentiable rounding control and error-guided constraints to proactively defend LLMs against quantization-conditioned backdoor attacks that bypass standard auditing.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 模型量化虽能降低LLM部署成本，但可能被利用构造量化后门攻击。本文提出QuantGuard，一种主动的量化前防御方法，通过可微舍入控制变量和误差引导的舍入反转约束，使得恶意行为在量化后无法激活。实验表明QuantGuard能有效阻止量化后门，同时保持模型性能。该工作为LLM部署安全提供了新的防御思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 模型量化虽能降低LLM部署成本，但可能被利用构造量化后门攻击。本文提出QuantGuard，一种主动的量化前防御方法，通过可微舍入控制变量和误差引导的舍入反转约束，使得恶意行为在量化后无法激活。实验表明QuantGuard能有效阻止量化后门，同时保持模型性能。该工作为LLM部署安全提供了新的防御思路。

quick_skim.title: Understanding and Evaluating Claw-like Agent Security Through a Computer-Systems Lens
quick_skim.abstract: Claw-like AI agents (e.g., OpenClaw) are always-on processes with persistent access to credentials, files, tools, and external services. They take on system-level responsibilities -- installing packages, maintaining state, scheduling subtasks, and mediating I/O -- making security failures far more severe than in other agents. Yet existing benchmarks focus on model responses and tool calls, leaving cross-component failure modes largely unmeasured. We adopt a computer-system analogy: treating a Claw-like agent as an agentic computer system whose gateway runtime plays an OS-like mediation role, whose Skills resemble user-installed applications, and whose Plugins resemble loadable extensions with runtime privileges. Each component has a classical counterpart whose protection mechanisms -- refined over decades of cybersecurity research -- are absent on the agent side. From this perspective, we develop SafeClawArena, a benchmark of 406 adversarial tasks across four attack surfaces (Skill Supply-Chain Integrity, Persistent State Exploitation, Cross-Boundary Data Flow, and Indirect Prompt Injection), executed in containerized replicas of real agent platforms with canary-marked credentials and evaluated via automated taint tracking across nine output channels. We evaluate three platforms (OpenClaw, NemoClaw, SeClaw) and five frontier LLMs. The highest attack success rate reaches 70%; malicious Plugins succeed in 100% of cases regardless of the LLM. SeClaw cuts GPT-5.4's attack success rate from 70% to 22%, partly through utility-security tradeoffs rather than active defenses, while Claude-Opus-4.6 already sits near a 22% floor on every platform. These results expose the inadequacy of current defenses and suggest directions for future hardening. Code and data: https://github.com/sunblaze-ucb/SafeClawArena.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Agent security analysis and evaluation via computer-systems analogy
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过计算机系统类比进行智能体安全分析与评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过计算机系统类比进行智能体安全分析与评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过计算机系统类比进行智能体安全分析与评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Analyzes Claw-like AI agent security by analogy to computer systems, identifying cross-component failure modes. Valuable for understanding safety mechanisms in LLM-based autonomous agents.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有智能体安全基准仅关注模型响应和工具调用，缺乏跨组件故障模式测量。本文采用计算机系统类比，将Claw类智能体视为代理计算机系统，分析其运行时、技能和插件的安全风险。该工作为理解LLM自主智能体的安全机制提供了新视角，但未提出具体防御方法。

quick_skim.title: The Past Is Prologue: A Plug-in Controller for Selective Updates in Sequentially Evolving LLM Memory
quick_skim.abstract: Sequentially evolving LLM memory enables agents to reuse past experience, but existing systems usually deploy each locally generated memory update without checking whether it improves future behavior. As a result, updates that help the current task may overwrite useful knowledge, introduce over-specific rules, or bias the final memory toward recent examples. We propose Janus, a plug-in memory controller that decides whether to accept a candidate memory update or retain the previous memory. To make this decision efficient, Janus uses a Memory Momentum Trigger to identify suspicious deviations in the memory-update trajectory, and compares old and new memories on a compact hybrid evaluation set of coverage, boundary, and fresh tasks instead of replaying the full history. Janus is method-agnostic and wraps existing updaters without changing their update rules. Across six datasets, two backbone LLMs, and two memory updaters, Janus improves average accuracy by +2.7 to +4.6 points over the corresponding base updaters.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: plug-in memory controller to prevent harmful updates in LLM agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于LLM智能体的内存控制器，防止有害更新
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于LLM智能体的内存控制器，防止有害更新
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于LLM智能体的内存控制器，防止有害更新
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Janus, a plug-in memory controller for LLM agents that selectively accepts or rejects candidate memory updates to prevent overwriting useful knowledge and overfitting.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该文针对LLM智能体顺序演化记忆中本地更新可能覆盖有用知识或引入偏置的问题，提出Janus插件控制器。Janus通过内存动量触发器检测可疑偏差，并在紧凑的混合评估集上比较新旧记忆，决定是否保留更新。这种方法无需重新训练，可插拔地提升记忆质量。实验表明Janus能有效防止记忆退化，提高智能体任务性能。
